title: Audit and shrink runtime primitive inventory

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Implement #1357 by making the generated-package inventory proof distinguish current/fresh generated targets from minimized runtime boundary status.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not relax the generated-from-IR parent intent. Do not treat whole runtime files or packages as acceptable command-behavior implementation surfaces. Keep generic generation support routed to command-generation and keep AW-owned primitive implementations exact and reviewable.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: How to summarize accepted runtime symbols, minimization status, and move-candidate routing inside existing check/proof surfaces.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A correct fix requires changing command-generation APIs, adding a new runtime module, or removing a runtime primitive whose behavior is not understood.
canonical_core.next_action: Inspect the runtime inventory/check implementation, add compact minimization reporting, and run focused proof.
completion_criteria: The generated-package proof distinguishes generated target freshness/completion from runtime-boundary minimization.
Accepted runtime boundaries are exact and reviewable by symbol/owner/reason rather than accepted as a whole category.
Move-to-command-generation or generated-command-behavior candidates are surfaced explicitly instead of hidden behind package-domain wording.
#1354 remains open unless later lanes satisfy parity/freshness and external command-generation support.

goal: Create a bounded plan for Audit and shrink runtime primitive inventory.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.outcome: Implement #1357 by making accepted runtime primitive inventory minimization explicit in generated-package proof.
intent.constraints: Keep scope inside existing generated-package inventory/check surfaces and do not soften the parent single-source boundary.
intent.latitude: Choose compact status fields, exact-symbol summaries, and tests that make the remaining runtime inventory reviewable.
intent.escalation: Escalate if the work requires new command-generation APIs, unsafe primitive removal, or a broader generated target migration.

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #1354 generated-CLI single-source boundary: runtime command behavior is generated from canonical IR/command-generation wherever applicable and hand-owned primitive runtime code stays minimal, exact, and justified.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After this lane closes, promote #1358 and any command-generation support lane needed for full #1354 satisfaction.

iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: yes; planning closeout recorded explicit proof input.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: None for this archived slice; reopen only if new evidence invalidates the proof.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Audit and shrink runtime primitive inventory
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Make the remaining AW-owned primitive runtime inventory exact, justified, and visibly transitional where it should later move toward generated/command-generation support.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Expose minimization status and exact accepted-boundary evidence in the generated-package blocker/proof report.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Check whether the report separates freshness from minimization and whether any accepted runtime boundary is still justified only by vague package-domain wording.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; src/agentic_workspace/contracts/python_runtime_projection_inventory.json; src/agentic_workspace/contracts/operation_primitives.json; tests/test_generated_command_package_proof_runner.py; planning closeout artifacts for this lane.
execution_bounds.max changed files: About 6 files; ask before touching generated targets or command-generation in this slice.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: Focused generated-package proof runner tests, generated package blocker JSON check, generated package static check, and generator --check.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before moving primitives, editing generated targets directly, or adding new command-generation APIs.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Generated target files, unrelated runtime primitives, broad command dispatch behavior, or TypeScript parity work reserved for #1358.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate exact accepted-boundary inventory and minimization status.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, #1357, the #1354 decomposition, the runtime inventory contracts, generated-package check script, and proof-runner tests.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Continue from #1357: expose runtime-boundary minimization status in generated-package proof without closing #1354.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Make #1357 runtime inventory minimization reviewable in generated-package proof.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Do not soften the generated CLI single-source boundary or substitute package-level acceptance for exact primitive evidence.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: The compact field names and exact report layout inside existing proof surfaces.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The fix requires unsafe primitive removal, new command-generation support, or broader parity/freshness work.

post_decomposition_delegation.decision rule: After this slice is bounded, decide whether direct work, read-only exploration, implementation handoff, validation handoff, or stronger review improves quality or saves tokens safely.

completion_criteria: Runtime-boundary minimization status is reported separately from generated target freshness/completion.
Accepted runtime boundaries are exact-symbol evidence with owner/reason/minimization metadata.
Generic/generated-command behavior candidates are visible and routed instead of hidden by broad package-domain acceptance.
Lane closeout keeps #1354 open for #1358 and command-generation support.
